title: scaevola kilaueae
intro: Scaevola kilaueae, also known as Kalo 'Ulu, is a species of flowering plant in the family Goodeniaceae, native to the Hawaiian Islands. It has been used in traditional Hawaiian medicine to treat various health conditions, including respiratory issues, skin irritations, and wounds, due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiseptic properties. The plant's health benefits include relieving coughs, soothing skin irritations, and promoting wound healing. In herbal preparations, Scaevola kilaueae is often used to make teas, tinctures, and salves, which can be used to address various health concerns.
